DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
accessibility artifact-reference example extensions guideline link metadata rich-text status usecase
anatomy api component design-specifications state variant
theme token-api token-value token
style
pattern

DSDS Accessibility Definitions

Definitions for documenting accessibility requirements, keyboard interactions, ARIA attributes, and color contrast.

Source: common/accessibility.schema.json

4 definitions in this file:

  • keyboardInteraction
  • ariaAttribute
  • colorContrast
  • accessibilityObject

keyboardInteraction

A keyboard interaction specification.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
keystringrequiredThe key or key combination (e.g., 'Enter', 'Space', 'Tab', 'Shift+Tab', 'Escape').
actionstringrequiredWhat happens when this key is pressed.
contextstringoptionalAny preconditions for this interaction (e.g., 'When the menu is open', 'When focus is on the trigger').

Example

[
  {
    "key": "Enter",
    "action": "Activates the button."
  },
  {
    "key": "Space",
    "action": "Activates the button."
  },
  {
    "key": "Tab",
    "action": "Moves focus to the next focusable element in the tab order."
  },
  {
    "key": "Shift+Tab",
    "action": "Moves focus to the previous focusable element in the tab order."
  },
  {
    "key": "Escape",
    "action": "Closes the dialog.",
    "context": "When focus is inside the dialog."
  }
]

ariaAttribute

Documentation for an ARIA attribute used by the artifact.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
attributestringrequiredThe ARIA attribute name (e.g., 'role', 'aria-label', 'aria-expanded', 'aria-disabled').
descriptionrichTextrequired
valuestringoptionalThe expected value or value pattern (e.g., 'true | false', 'string', 'button').
requiredbooleanoptionalWhether this attribute is always required on the artifact.

References: richText

Example

[
  {
    "attribute": "role",
    "value": "button",
    "description": "Applied automatically by the <button> element. Only set explicitly when using a non-button element.",
    "required": false
  },
  {
    "attribute": "aria-disabled",
    "value": "true | false",
    "description": "Set to true when the button is non-interactive. Preferred over the HTML disabled attribute when the button should remain focusable for screen reader discoverability.",
    "required": false
  },
  {
    "attribute": "aria-label",
    "value": "string",
    "description": "Provides an accessible name for icon-only buttons that lack visible text.",
    "required": false
  },
  {
    "attribute": "aria-expanded",
    "value": "true | false",
    "description": "Indicates whether a controlled element (menu, dialog, tooltip) is currently expanded.",
    "required": false
  }
]

colorContrast

A color contrast specification documenting the contrast ratio between a foreground and background color pair.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
foregroundstringrequiredThe foreground token name or resolved color value (e.g., 'color-text-primary', '#1a1a1a').
backgroundstringrequiredThe background token name or resolved color value (e.g., 'color-background-default', '#ffffff').
contrastRationumberrequiredThe contrast ratio as a decimal number (e.g., 4.5, 7.2, 15.3). Represents the ratio N:1 where N is this value. Tools can format for display as 'N:1'.
level"A" | "AA" | "AAA"requiredThe WCAG conformance level met by this contrast ratio.
contextstringoptionalWhere this foreground/background combination is used (e.g., 'Label text on primary button background in light mode').

Example

[
  {
    "foreground": "color-text-on-action",
    "background": "color-action-primary",
    "contrastRatio": 7.2,
    "level": "AAA",
    "context": "Label text on primary button background in light mode."
  },
  {
    "foreground": "color-text-on-action",
    "background": "color-action-danger",
    "contrastRatio": 6.5,
    "level": "AAA",
    "context": "Label text on danger button background in light mode."
  },
  {
    "foreground": "color-action-primary",
    "background": "color-background-default",
    "contrastRatio": 4.8,
    "level": "AA",
    "context": "Secondary button border and text against the default page background."
  }
]

accessibilityObject

Explicit accessibility documentation for an artifact — keyboard behavior, ARIA attributes, screen reader announcements, focus management, color contrast, and motion considerations. This object is reserved for structured accessibility specifications that have their own dedicated schemas (keyboard interactions, ARIA attributes, color contrast pairs). For accessibility *guidelines* (actionable rules with rationale), add them to the artifact's top-level `guidelines` array with `category` set to 'accessibility'. This separation keeps the accessibility object focused on machine-readable specifications while guidelines remain in a single, filterable array.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
wcagLevel"A" | "AA" | "AAA"optionalThe minimum WCAG conformance level targeted by this artifact.
keyboardInteractionkeyboardInteraction[]optionalKeyboard interaction specifications.
ariaAttributesariaAttribute[]optionalARIA attribute documentation.
screenReaderBehaviorrichTextoptional
focusManagementrichTextoptional
colorContrastcolorContrast[]optionalContrast ratio documentation for foreground/background color pairs.
motionConsiderationsrichTextoptional

References: keyboardInteraction, ariaAttribute, richText, colorContrast

Example

{
  "wcagLevel": "AA",
  "keyboardInteraction": [
    {
      "key": "Enter",
      "action": "Activates the button."
    },
    {
      "key": "Space",
      "action": "Activates the button."
    },
    {
      "key": "Tab",
      "action": "Moves focus to the next focusable element."
    }
  ],
  "ariaAttributes": [
    {
      "attribute": "aria-disabled",
      "value": "true | false",
      "description": "Set to true when the button is non-interactive.",
      "required": false
    },
    {
      "attribute": "aria-label",
      "value": "string",
      "description": "Provides an accessible name for icon-only buttons.",
      "required": false
    }
  ],
  "screenReaderBehavior": "Announced as '[label], button'. When disabled via aria-disabled, announced as '[label], button, dimmed' (VoiceOver) or '[label], button, unavailable' (NVDA/JAWS). When loading, announced as '[label], button, busy'.",
  "focusManagement": "The button participates in the normal tab order. It does not trap or redirect focus. When the button triggers a modal, focus is moved to the opened element by the modal component, not the button.",
  "colorContrast": [
    {
      "foreground": "color-text-on-action",
      "background": "color-action-primary",
      "contrastRatio": 7.2,
      "level": "AAA",
      "context": "Label text on primary button background in light mode."
    }
  ],
  "motionConsiderations": "The loading spinner animation respects the prefers-reduced-motion media query. When reduced motion is preferred, the spinner is replaced with a static ellipsis indicator."
}
↑ Back to top

Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) 0.1 — Draft Specification
